caption: Facebook News Feed Requirements
context: Here's how it might look on your whiteboard:

caption: Simple Post Creation
context: Users hit our API gateway with a new post request, this gets passed to one of the post service endpoints which creates an insert event that is sent to our database. Easy.

caption: Following Service
context: This allows us to query for the important pieces:

caption: Naive Feed Service
context: We'll start by adding a feed service to do this work. It's going to be read-heavy and have very different query patterns than the Post and Follow services, so separating it out makes sense.

caption: Adding a Feed Table
context: 1) How do we handle users who are following a large number of users?

caption: Async Workers for Post Creates
context: Any queue will work here so long as it support at-least-once delivery of messages and is highly scalable. Amazon's Simple Queue Service (SQS) will work great here.

caption: Async Workers for Post Creates
context: A great option would extend on Async Workers outlined above. We'll create async feed workers that are working off a shared queue to write to our precomputed feeds.

caption: Distributed Post Cache
context: When posts are edited (not created!) we simply invalidate the cache for that post ID.

caption: Hot key issue
context: We can use Redis for this and key off the post ID so we can evenly distribute posts across our cluster.

caption: Redundant Post Cache
context: Both solve the problem, but this solution means we have N times the throughput for a hot key without any additional coordination required.
